Quarterly Revenue by Region
Region Q1 Q2 Q3 Q4
North 10,000 12,000 11,000 14,000
South 9,500 11,500 10,300 12,800
East 10,000 12,000 12,800 16,300
Total 29,500 35,500 34,100 43,100
`; };

Region Q1 Q2 Q3 Q4
North 12,400 15,200 14,800 18,300
South 9,800 11,400 10,200 13,600
East 7,300 8,900 9,100 11,200
Total 29,500 35,500 34,100 43,100

export const html04ArticleWithStructuredMetadata = (): string => { return ` Designing Reliable Frontend Benchmarks

Designing Reliable Frontend Benchmarks

November 15, 2024

By Alex Morgan
145 Market Street, Suite 500, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

Benchmarking frontend systems is easy to start and hard to do well. Without clear methodology, minor environment changes can swamp meaningful differences between implementations.

This article outlines a repeatable process: isolate variables, fix test data, automate warm-ups, and publish raw run outputs alongside summary statistics.

Filed under: Performance Engineering, Tooling

`; };

export const html04ArticleWithStructuredMetadata = (): string => { return ` Designing Reliable Frontend Benchmarks

Designing Reliable Frontend Benchmarks

November 15, 2024

By Alex Morgan
145 Market Street, Suite 500, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA

Benchmarking frontend systems is easy to start and hard to do well. Without clear methodology, minor environment changes can swamp meaningful differences between implementations.

This article outlines a repeatable process: isolate variables, fix test data, automate warm-ups, and publish raw run outputs alongside summary statistics.

Filed under: Performance Engineering, Tooling

`; };

export const html04ArticleWithStructuredMetadata = (): string => { return ` Building Accessible Interfaces with Semantic HTML

Building Accessible Interfaces with Semantic HTML

Published November 15, 2024

By Alex Porter
Semantic Labs, 101 Web Way, Austin, TX

Semantic markup improves interoperability with assistive technologies and clarifies document intent.

Combining native elements with structured metadata supports discoverability and consistent indexing.

Filed under Accessibility, HTML, and Web Standards.

`; };

export const html07DefinitionListGlossary = (): string => { return `

Technical Glossary

  • API
  • CSS
  • DOM
  • Latency
  • Refactor
API
A defined set of rules that allows different software systems to communicate with each other.
CSS
A stylesheet language used to control the visual presentation and layout of web documents.
DOM
A programming interface that represents an HTML or XML document as a tree of objects.
Latency
The time delay between a user action and the corresponding response from a system or network.
Refactor
The process of restructuring existing code to improve readability or maintainability without changing behavior.
`; };

export const html07DefinitionListGlossary = (): string => { return `

Technical Glossary

  • API
  • CSS
  • DOM
  • Latency
  • Refactor
API
A defined set of rules that allows different software systems to communicate with each other.
CSS
A stylesheet language used to control the visual presentation and layout of web documents.
DOM
A programming interface that represents an HTML or XML document as a tree of objects.
Latency
The time delay between a user action and the corresponding response from a system or network.
Refactor
The process of restructuring existing code to improve readability or maintainability without changing behavior.
`; };

export const html07DefinitionListGlossary = (): string => { return `

Frontend glossary

  • API
  • DOM
  • LCP
  • A11y
  • SSR
API
A contract that allows software systems to communicate and exchange data.
DOM
The in-memory representation of an HTML document that scripts can inspect and modify.
LCP
Largest Contentful Paint, a Core Web Vital focused on loading performance.
A11y
A shorthand for accessibility practices that make interfaces usable for everyone.
SSR
Server-side rendering, where HTML is generated on the server before reaching the browser.
`; };

export const html09SemanticPageSkeleton = (): string => { return ` Semantic Page Skeleton Skip to main content
Orbit Journal
  • Stories
  • Topics
  • About

How semantic landmarks improve navigation

Why landmarks matter

Landmarks help assistive technologies jump between major areas quickly.

Practical rollout plan

Start by replacing generic wrappers with meaningful sectioning elements.

Related resources

  • ARIA basics
  • Semantic audit checklist
  • Privacy
  • Terms
  • Contact

© 2026 Orbit Journal

`; };

results.error:
The input did not match the regular expression /capacity must be a positive integer/. Input:

'Error: Invalid capacity'

Expected values to be strictly equal:
+ actual - expected

+ 1867108634
- 1009084850

Expected values to be strictly deep-equal:
+ actual - expected

  [
+   -0,
    0,
-   0,
    9,
+   -0,
    0,
-   0
  ]

The input did not match the regular expression /(?:Error:\s*)?k\s+(?:is\s+)?(?:out\s+of\s+)?range/i. Input:

'RangeError: k is out of bounds'

The input did not match the regular expression /export\s+function\s+buildQueryString\s*\(\s*params\s*:\s*[^)]*(string\s*\|\s*number\s*\|\s*boolean|boolean\s*\|\s*number\s*\|\s*string|number\s*\|\s*boolean\s*\|\s*string)/u. Input:

"import axios from 'axios';\n" +
  '\n' +
  'type PrimitiveQueryValue = string | number | boolean;\n' +
  'type HeadersMap = Record<string, string>;\n' +
  '\n' +
  'interface ConfigureOptions {\n' +
  '\tbaseURL: string;\n' +
  '\theaders?: HeadersMap;\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'interface PostOptions {\n' +
  '\theaders?: HeadersMap;\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'interface ParsedItem {\n' +
  '\tid: unknown;\n' +
  '\tlabel: string;\n' +
  '\tmeta: Record<string, unknown>;\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  "let baseURL = '';\n" +
  'const defaultHeaders: HeadersMap = {};\n' +
  '\n' +
  'function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {\n' +
  "\treturn typeof value === 'object' && value !== null;\n" +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'export function configure(options: ConfigureOptions): void {\n' +
  '\tbaseURL = options.baseURL;\n' +
  '\tif (options.headers) {\n' +
  '\t\tObject.assign(defaultHeaders, options.headers);\n' +
  '\t}\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'export async function get<T>(path: string, params?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T> {\n' +
  '\tconst response = await axios.get<T>(baseURL + path, {\n' +
  '\t\tparams,\n' +
  '\t\theaders: defaultHeaders,\n' +
  '\t});\n' +
  '\treturn response.data;\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'export async function post<T>(path: string, body?: unknown, options?: PostOptions): Promise<T> {\n' +
  '\tconst response = await axios.post<T>(baseURL + path, body, {\n' +
  '\t\theaders: {\n' +
  '\t\t\t...defaultHeaders,\n' +
  '\t\t\t...(options && options.headers),\n' +
  '\t\t},\n' +
  '\t});\n' +
  '\treturn response.data;\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'export function buildQueryString(params: Record<string, PrimitiveQueryValue | null | undefined>): string {\n' +
  '\treturn Object.entries(params)\n' +
  '\t\t.filter(([, v]) => v !== undefined && v !== null)\n' +
  '\t\t.map(([k, v]) => `${encodeURIComponent(k)}=${encodeURIComponent(String(v))}`)\n' +
  "\t\t.join('&');\n" +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'export function parseResponse(raw: unknown): ParsedItem[] {\n' +
  '\tif (!isRecord(raw)) {\n' +
  '\t\treturn [];\n' +
  '\t}\n' +
  '\tconst data = raw.data;\n' +
  '\tif (!isRecord(data)) {\n' +
  '\t\treturn [];\n' +
  '\t}\n' +
  '\tconst items = data.items;\n' +
  '\tif (!Array.isArray(items)) {\n' +
  '\t\treturn [];\n' +
  '\t}\n' +
  '\n' +
  '\treturn items.map((item) => {\n' +
  '\t\tlet id: unknown;\n' +
  "\t\tlet label = 'Unknown';\n" +
  '\t\tlet meta: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n' +
  '\n' +
  '\t\tif (isRecord(item)) {\n' +
  '\t\t\tid = item.id;\n' +
  '\n' +
  '\t\t\tconst name = item.name;\n' +
  '\t\t\tconst title = item.title;\n' +
  '\t\t\tconst itemMeta = item.meta;\n' +
  '\n' +
  "\t\t\tif (typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0) {\n" +
  '\t\t\t\tlabel = name;\n' +
  "\t\t\t} else if (typeof title === 'string' && title.length > 0) {\n" +
  '\t\t\t\tlabel = title;\n' +
  '\t\t\t}\n' +
  '\n' +
  '\t\t\tif (isRecord(itemMeta)) {\n' +
  '\t\t\t\tmeta = itemMeta;\n' +
  '\t\t\t}\n' +
  '\t\t}\n' +
  '\n' +
  '\t\treturn {\n' +
  '\t\t\tid,\n' +
  '\t\t\tlabel,\n' +
  '\t\t\tmeta,\n' +
  '\t\t};\n' +
  '\t});\n' +
  '}'

Expected values to be strictly equal:

false !== true

Expected values to be strictly equal:
+ actual - expected

+ 'First line content\n  that is quite\n  long indeed'
- 'First line content\n  that is quite long\n  indeed'

The input did not match the regular expression /typeof\s+payload\s*!==\s*'object'/. Input:

'export function readName(payload: unknown): string {\n' +
  '\tif (\n' +
  '\t\ttypeof payload === "object" &&\n' +
  '\t\tpayload !== null &&\n' +
  '\t\t"name" in payload &&\n' +
  '\t\ttypeof payload.name === "string"\n' +
  '\t) {\n' +
  '\t\treturn payload.name.trim();\n' +
  '\t}\n' +
  '\n' +
  '\tthrow new Error("Invalid payload: expected an object with a string name field.");\n' +
  '}'

The input did not match the regular expression /(?:payload\.)?id must be a string/i. Input:

'TypeError: Invalid payload: expected an object with string id'

The input was expected to not match the regular expression /PropertyDescriptor/u. Input:

'function log(target: object, key: string, descriptor: PropertyDescriptor): PropertyDescriptor {\n' +
  '\tconst original = descriptor.value as (...args: unknown[]) => unknown;\n' +
  '\tdescriptor.value = function (this: unknown, ...args: unknown[]) {\n' +
  '\t\tconsole.log(`[${key}] called with`, args);\n' +
  '\t\tconst result = original.apply(this, args);\n' +
  '\t\tconsole.log(`[${key}] returned`, result);\n' +
  '\t\treturn result;\n' +
  '\t};\n' +
  '\treturn descriptor;\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'class Calculator {\n' +
  '\tadd(a: number, b: number): number {\n' +
  '\t\treturn a + b;\n' +
  '\t}\n' +
  '}\n' +
  '\n' +
  'const addDescriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(Calculator.prototype, "add");\n' +
  'if (addDescriptor) {\n' +
  '\tObject.defineProperty(Calculator.prototype, "add", log(Calculator.prototype, "add", addDescriptor));\n' +
  '}'
